description: FRESH HOLDOUT — BurntSushi/ripgrep @ d5b85d44 (2026-07-09), Rust CLI text-search tool: different org and different domain than the burned httpx set. Cases authored 2026-07-13 AFTER the language-general supersession PR was frozen at ab8505e; single run, published as-is, no code or threshold changes after seeing results. U1–U6 are synthetic team-decision facts layered on the real corpus (topics collide with real docs: --max-columns, threads, config file, file types). R1–R5 are REAL changes verified in CHANGELOG.md / git history (deprecated flag replacements, hyperlink introduction, MSRV bump, empty -vf semantics) planted as old/new qa pairs — the real corpus (CHANGELOG, docs, 500 indexed commits) provides genuine corroboration and distractor pressure. Probes mix EN→EN and KO→EN (U5, U6, ADV3, A8, A9, P4 are Korean). abstention topics verified grep-absent (0 hits, 2026-07-13); present controls verified present.

old.query: what max columns setting does our shared ripgrep config use
old.answer: Our shared rg config file sets --max-columns=150 so long minified lines get elided in code search results.

new.query: max columns raised in shared config
new.answer: We raised --max-columns from 150 to 300 in the shared config because SQL fixtures kept getting truncated. The 150 value is obsolete.

update_cases.probe: what is our max-columns setting for rg?

old.query: how is case sensitivity configured in our rg alias
old.answer: The team alias forces --case-sensitive always; smart case was considered too surprising for code review greps.

new.query: rg alias switched to smart case
new.answer: The alias now uses --smart-case: lowercase patterns match case-insensitively, mixed-case patterns stay sensitive. The forced --case-sensitive alias is retired.

update_cases.probe: does our rg alias use smart case or case sensitive?

old.query: where does RIPGREP_CONFIG_PATH point on our dev boxes
old.answer: RIPGREP_CONFIG_PATH points to ~/.rgrc on every dev machine, provisioned by the dotfiles repo.

new.query: ripgrep config moved to xdg path
new.answer: The config moved from ~/.rgrc to ~/.config/ripgrep/rc to follow XDG conventions; RIPGREP_CONFIG_PATH now points there. The ~/.rgrc path is dead.

update_cases.probe: where is our ripgrep config file now?

update_cases.topic: custom type filters in search script

old.query: which file types does the code search script include
old.answer: The search script passes --type rust --type md only; web assets are excluded from indexed greps.

new.query: web type added to search script
new.answer: We added --type-add 'web:*.{html,css,js}' and --type web to the search script, so web assets are now searched alongside rust and md. The rust+md-only setup is outdated.

update_cases.probe: which file types does our search script cover?

old.query: why do we cap ripgrep threads
old.answer: CI runs rg with -j4 to cap CPU usage so the runner does not starve parallel jobs.

new.answer: The -j4 cap was removed after moving CI to dedicated runners; rg now picks thread count automatically. The -j4 setting no longer exists.

update_cases.probe: 우리 CI에서 rg 스레드 개수 제한 어떻게 하고 있지?

old.query: how do we handle binary files in repo-wide greps
old.answer: Repo-wide greps pass -a/--text so binary files are searched as text; we needed it for the fixture blobs.

new.query: text flag dropped from repo greps
new.answer: We dropped -a/--text from repo-wide greps and rely on ripgrep's default binary detection; the fixture blobs got proper .gitattributes instead. Searching binaries as text is no longer done.

update_cases.probe: 바이너리 파일 검색 처리 지금 어떻게 하지?

old.query: which flag makes ripgrep fall back to pcre2 automatically
old.answer: Use --auto-hybrid-regex: it automatically falls back to PCRE2 when the pattern needs features the default engine lacks (added in 11.0.0, #1155).

new.answer: --auto-hybrid-regex is deprecated as of 12.0.0. Use --engine auto instead — the --engine flag (#1488) switches between regex engines and its auto value replaces the old flag.

update_cases.probe: what flag gives automatic fallback to PCRE2?

old.query: how do i turn off unicode for pcre2 patterns
old.answer: Pass --no-pcre2-unicode to disable Unicode mode in PCRE2 patterns.

new.answer: --no-pcre2-unicode is deprecated. Use --no-unicode, which applies to both the default regex engine and PCRE2; the old flag remains only as an alias and may be removed.

update_cases.probe: how do I disable unicode matching for pcre2 patterns?

old.query: can ripgrep emit clickable file links in the terminal
old.answer: No — ripgrep has no hyperlink support; file paths are plain text in the output.

new.answer: ripgrep 14.0.0 added opt-in terminal hyperlinks via --hyperlink-format (#665): use --hyperlink-format default, or --hyperlink-format vscode to open results in VS Code. Plain-text-only output is no longer the whole story.

update_cases.topic: REAL: minimum supported Rust version bump (CHANGELOG 11.0.0)

old.query: what rust version do we need to build ripgrep
old.answer: ripgrep compiles with Rust 1.28.0 as the minimum supported version.

new.answer: The minimum supported Rust version increased from 1.28.0 to 1.34.0 in the 11.0.0 release; 1.28.0 no longer builds it.

update_cases.probe: what is the minimum supported Rust version to build?

old.query: what does rg -vf do with an empty pattern file
old.answer: rg -vf file with an empty file matches nothing — no patterns means no lines survive the inverted match.

new.answer: As of 15.0.0, rg -vf file where the file is empty now matches everything: inverting zero patterns leaves every line. The old matches-nothing behavior is gone.

update_cases.probe: what happens when rg -vf gets an empty pattern file?

adversarial_recency_cases.probe: how does gitignore precedence work across nested directories?

exact.query: how does rg decide gitignore precedence
exact.answer: Deeper .gitignore files take precedence over shallower ones, and later rules in a file override earlier ones; global gitignore has the lowest precedence.

adjacent.answer: A memory usage regression when handling very large gitignore files was fixed by interning glob patterns.

adversarial_recency_cases.probe: how do we exclude the target directory from searches?

exact.query: how do we exclude target dirs in the search script
exact.answer: The search script passes --glob '!target/**' so build output never shows up in results.

adjacent.answer: Shell completions for glob-taking flags were improved in zsh and fish; completion now suggests flag values.

adversarial_recency_cases.probe: vscode 터미널에서 rg 하이퍼링크 포맷 뭐로 설정하지?

exact.query: which hyperlink format do we use for vscode terminals
exact.answer: Dev containers set --hyperlink-format vscode so clicking a result opens the file in VS Code at the matched line.

adjacent.answer: The repo's .vscode/extensions.json now recommends rust-analyzer and even-better-toml for new contributors.

abstention_cases.query: How does the Kafka consumer group handle rebalancing?
Where is the OAuth token refresh implemented?
What Kubernetes resources does the deployment create?
How does the websocket reconnect logic work?
How does cursor-based pagination work in the list API?
결제 PG 이중화 구성이 어떻게 되어 있지?

present_controls.query: How do glob patterns include and exclude files?
How do I enable PCRE2 for lookaround patterns?
